Water Utility Turnaround Rapid Diagnostic Framework

Professional Assessment Tool
Input parameters
Non-revenue water (NRW) 35%
Physical losses (leakage, bursts) + commercial losses (illegal connections, meter inaccuracies, billing errors) expressed as a percentage of water introduced into the distribution system.
Revenue collection efficiency 72%
Proportion of billed revenue actually received as cash by the utility.
O&D cost reserve 45%
Percentage of revenue required for operations, maintenance, asset renewal and development costs, excluding staff salaries.
Data confidence level
Audited = verified data  ·  Estimated = calculated from partial data  ·  Assumed = proxy figures used
Utility A
NRW 25%
Collection efficiency 85%
O&D reserve 35%
Utility B
NRW 48%
Collection efficiency 60%
O&D reserve 60%
WTRDF utility health matrix
CSI range Classification Business condition Turnaround intensity Category Ext. fin.
Above 75% Healthy Strong commercial sustainability No turnaround needed Cat. 1 0–5%
65–75% Stable but Vulnerable Sustainable but exposed to risk Light optimisation Cat. 2 5–15%
50–65% Distressed Significant revenue leakage Structured turnaround Cat. 3 15–40%
35–50% Severely Distressed Serious commercial weaknesses Major turnaround programme Cat. 4 40–70%
Below 35% Critical Commercially unsustainable Emergency intervention Cat. 5 70–100%+
Operational Sustainability Threshold (OST): CSI ≥ 70% — utilities above this threshold are generally capable of funding operations from internally generated resources. The CSI must exceed the OMAD (Operations, Maintenance, Asset Renewal and Development) requirement for long-term sustainability.
Four-stage value chain model
Stage 1
Water production
100% of value created
Stage 2
NRW losses
Physical + commercial losses
Stage 3
Collection gap
Uncollected billed revenue
Stage 4
Net CSI
After O&D cost reservation
Core formula: CSI = (1 − NRW) × Collection Efficiency   |   Extended: Net CSI = CSI × (1 − O&D Reserve %)
Seven-stage assessment process
Stage 1
WTRDF Rapid Diagnostic
Stage 2
Technical Assessment
Stage 3
Commercial Assessment
Stage 4
Financial Assessment
Stage 5
Institutional & Governance
Stage 6
Turnaround Strategy
Stage 7
Implementation & Monitoring
